title: To One Who Ran Down The English
content:
You make our faults too gross, and thence maintain
Our darker future. May your fears be vain!
At times the small black fly upon the pane
May seem the black ox of the distant plain.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alfred Lord Tennyson, titled "To One Who Ran Down The English", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
